channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Schumer seeks legislation giving local officials authority to ‘swiftly’ respond to drone sightings
item.description: Sen. Chuck Schumer, a Democrat from New York, is seeking to have legislation passed that provides local officials greater authority to respond to possible drone sightings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate Democrats push plan to abolish Electoral College
item.description: Three Democratic senators unveiled a constitutional amendment to abolish the Electoral College system Monday, just more than a month after President-elect Trump stunned the Democrats by sweeping all seven battleground states, knocking off three Senate Democratic incumbents in the process. Sens.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Two charged in connection with Iran-backed drone strike that killed 3 US troops in the Middle East
item.description: Two men, including a dual Iranian American citizen, have been arrested on charges that they exported sensitive technology to Iran that was used in a drone attack in Jordan that killed three American troops early this year and injured dozens of other servicemembers.
item.dc:creator: Steve Leblanc, Eric Tucker And Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DC councilman recommended for expulsion as he faces bribery charges
item.description: Washington, D.C. city councilman Trayon White could be removed from office after an ad hoc committee unanimously voted on Monday to recommend the council expel him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: First on Fox: Trump Small Business Administration pick Loeffler to meet with GOP senators
item.description: Former Sen. Kelly Loeffler of Georgia will make her first visit to Capitol Hill since President-elect Trump nominated her to steer the Small Business Administration in his second term in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: March for Our Lives co-founder David Hogg launches DNC vice chair bid
item.description: Hogg, 24, cited Democrats’ losing ground with young voters in November as a motivating factor to run for the leadership position within the party.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he's a 'believer' in polio vaccine, and other news conference takeaways
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Monday held a wide-ranging news conference in which he said he would preserve access to the polio vaccine but equivocated on other vaccines, pledged to look at bringing down the costs of pharmaceuticals and expressed doubts that his daughter-in-law might be Florida's
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics: The DNC's Blue Christmas
item.description: Welcome to the Fox News Politics newsletter, featuring the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top Republican demands answers from Zuckerberg, accusing Meta of ‘shadow banning’ military content
item.description: Sen. Joni Ernst, R-Iowa, said in a letter to Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg that he needs to answer for purported "shadow banning" of military-related postings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What's behind rising autism rates: A broader definition of autism and better screening
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump seemed to entertain the discredited theory that vaccines cause autism as he answered questions from journalists Monday at his Mar-a-Lago club.
item.dc:creator: Carla K. Johnson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says he will consider pardon for NYC Mayor Eric Adams, says he was targeted for illegal migrant stance
item.description: President-elect Trump said he would consider issuing a pardon for New York City Mayor Eric Adams amid corruption charges alleging he took bribes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump weighs in on NYC mayor, vaccines and drones in freewheeling press conference at Mar-a-Lago
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump told reporters that he would consider pardoning embattled New York Mayor Eric Adams, said the country was “not going to lose” the polio vaccine and weighed in on the flurry of drone sightings over New Jersey during a freewheeling press conference Monday at his Mar-a-Lago
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court declines appeal from Peter Navarro; Trump White House aide to return after prison term
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court turned back an appeal Monday from Peter Navarro, the former White House official who is set to return in Donald Trump's second term after serving prison time on contempt of Congress charges.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former FBI informant pleads guilty to lying about phony bribery scheme involving the Bidens
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — A former FBI informant pleaded guilty on Monday to lying about a phony bribery scheme involving President Joe Biden and his son Hunter that became central to the Republican impeachment inquiry in Congress.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden admin expected to offer California waiver to ban gas-powered cars by 2035: report
item.description: The Biden administration is reportedly considering giving California permission to ban new gas-powered vehicles in the state within a decade before the Democrat's term comes to an end.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC Mayor Adams denied public funds for 2025 reelection campaign: CFB
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams was denied public funds for his 2025 reelection campaign, the New York City Campaign Finance Board announced on Monday. The CFB cited the federal charges against Adams as one of the reasons why it denied him the money.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Donald Trump says he would consider pardoning NYC Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) -- President-elect Donald Trump on Monday said he would consider pardoning Mayor Eric Adams after his historic indictment on federal charges. "I think that he was treated pretty unfairly," Trump said at a press conference in Florida. "I would certainly look at it.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Paris Hilton heading to Capitol with 'urgent' plea to pass youth welfare program reform
item.description: Paris Hilton is heading back to the Capitol with an urgent plea, calling on "every member of the House" to pass a bill focused on youth congregate care facilities that she says would "create real, meaningful change" before the legislative session ends this week.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Report documents family separations during 1st Trump term
item.description: A report examining years of family separation policies enacted under the Trump administration is being released Monday by several migrant advocacy nonprofit organizations to remind the public and lawmakers of what transpired, Border Report has learned.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5

item.title: Youth movement among House Democrats is overrated
item.description: The youth movement among House Democrats? Don't believe the hype. Even as a few Democratic committee heads are being pushed aside for younger replacements, the party is elevating some of its most senior members to lead virtually every major committee in the next Congress.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson faces 'complicated' decision on crucial Rules Committee chair
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is barreling into a tricky — yet critical — decision over whom to appoint as the next chair of the House Rules Committee, a post that will set the tone for legislating and play a key role in keeping Republicans united in next year’s ultrathin majority.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Senator Schumer urges use of ROBIN to track mystery drones
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Sen. Chuck Schumer is making a plea for federal officials to launch a declassified special drone detection system called the ROBIN – a 360-degree technology across New York, New Jersey and the metro area to track any unidentified object in the sky.
item.dc:creator: Grace Gomez, PIX 11

item.title: Lawmakers struggle to reach deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional negotiators are struggling to reach a deal to keep the government's lights on past Dec. 20. Text of a continuing resolution (CR) for a government funding package was expected Sunday ahead of the looming shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pelosi 'on the mend' after hip replacement surgery in Germany
item.description: "Speaker Pelosi is enjoying the overwhelming outpouring of prayers and well wishes," Ian Krager said.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, NewsNation

item.title: ABC News to pay $15M in Trump defamation lawsuit
item.description: Court documents publicly filed on Saturday and obtained by NewsNation detailed the settlement's conditions.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Biden urges Congress to 'do something' for gun reform on Sandy Hook anniversary
item.description: Biden in a press release touted his administration's efforts to combat gun violence but argued that there was more to be done — especially as his presidency enters the final stretch.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, close allies attend Army-Navy rivalry game
item.description: As national concerns about the drones spotted on the East Coast continue to mount, President-elect Trump is attending the Army-Navy game.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese phone hack victims haven't been notified: Report
item.description: AT&T and Verizon have reportedly not informed some hacking victims that they were specifically targeted.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Frey issues another veto, rejecting council decision to deny raises for high paid city staff
item.description: Mayor Jacob Frey issued his third veto of the month Thursday, objecting to the Minneapolis City Council’s decision to withhold raises from city employees as a means of reducing the property tax levy increase.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Kimberly Guilfoyle is the latest Trump family ally chosen for an ambassadorship
item.description: Kimberly Guilfoyle, who got engaged to Trump's son in 2020, is the latest appointee he's chosen from his family tree. There's a long and controversial history of presidents picking relatives for jobs.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman, MPR News

item.title: Florida state lawmaker files measure eliminating tuition aid for undocumented immigrants
item.description: Florida state Sen. Randy Fine (R) announced Monday he was introducing a bill to limit college tuition aid to undocumented immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: DA suggests judge could permanently halt Trump’s hush money case while upholding his conviction
item.description: Prosecutors are urging a judge not to throw out President-elect Donald Trump’s hush money criminal conviction but suggesting a willingness to end the case in a way that would preserve the verdict while avoiding a protracted legal fight.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump-era Justice Department subpoenaed congressional staffers, watchdog finds
item.description: But the department's internal watchdog found no evidence of political motivation by federal prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Biden is rushing aid to Ukraine. Both sides are digging in. And everyone is bracing for Trump
item.description: The grinding war pitting Ukraine against its Russian invaders has escalated ahead of Donald Trump’s inauguration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
